title: Neopaxia racemosa
intro:

However, I found a plant called Neopaxia (Neopaxia racemosa is not found) but a plant called 'Neopanax' is not found  but the Neopanax spp. and the 'Neopanax'  are often confused with the 'Neopanax arboreus' also known as the "Grape Holly" or "Makomako" (Neopanax arboreus is a plant in the family Araliaceae and is native to New Zealand). 

Here's some general information about Araliaceae. 

Araliaceae is a family of flowering plants, which include the Neopanax arboreus.

The health benefits of Araliaceae plants include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties.

The most common herbal preparations made with Araliaceae plants include extracts and infusions, often used to support immune function and overall health.

taxonomy: Neopaxia racemosa is a plant species within the family Boraginaceae. It is a genus of plants that are commonly found in the southwestern United States, Mexico, and parts of Central America. The plant Neopaxia racemosa is classified as a flowering plant, specifically a shrub or subshrub. Neopaxia racemosa is further classified as a member of the subfamily Cynogloeinae. This classification system helps to group and categorize Neopaxia racemosa and other similar plant species.
